mode_switching_guide.overview: This mode focuses on widget implementation. When tasks move beyond widget authoring into packaging or deployment, explicit handoff to the package manager mode is required.

scenario.trigger: Widget implementation is complete and user requests packaging
scenario.reason: Packaging workflows, TWX generation, and deployment coordination belong to the package manager mode, not the widget authoring mode.

example_phrases.phrase: "Package this widget into a TWX file"

scenario.trigger: User asks about packaging configuration or TWX structure
scenario.reason: Questions about packaging mechanics, toolkit configuration, or TWX artifact structure are outside widget implementation scope.

scenario.trigger: User requests validation of packaging readiness
scenario.reason: Packaging validation involves checking widget structure against packaging requirements, which is the package manager's responsibility.

practice.name: Complete current widget work before switching
practice.description: Ensure the widget implementation is in a coherent state before handing off to packaging. Don't leave partial implementations.

practice.name: Provide clear context in switch reason
practice.description: When switching modes, explain what was completed and what the user is requesting next, so the package manager mode has full context.
practice.good_example: "Widget implementation complete. User requesting packaging of Stepper widget into TWX."

practice.name: Don't attempt packaging operations before switching
practice.description: Resist the temptation to "help" with packaging tasks. Switch modes cleanly rather than blurring responsibilities.

question.q: Should I validate widget structure before completing?
question.a: Yes, validate that widget files are consistent and complete. But don't validate against packaging requirements - that's the package manager's job.

question.q: Can I mention packaging in completion messages?
question.a: You can acknowledge that the widget is ready for packaging, but don't provide packaging instructions or suggest packaging commands.

question.q: What if the user asks about both widget changes and packaging?
question.a: Complete the widget changes first, then switch to package manager mode for the packaging portion of the request.
